steps.title: Square both sides and rearrange
steps.content: Square $\sqrt{k - x} = 57 - x$ to clear the radical: $$k - x = (57 - x)^2 = 3249 - 114x + x^2.$$ Rearranging: $$x^2 - 113x + (3249 - k) = 0.$$

steps.title: Set discriminant = 0 for exactly one real solution
steps.content: The discriminant is $\Delta = 113^2 - 4(3249 - k) = 4k - 227$. For exactly one solution, set $\Delta = 0$: $$4k - 227 = 0 \implies 4k = 227.$$ The repeated root $x = 56.5$ satisfies $x \leq 57$, so it is valid. That matches choice 227 — 227 is correct.
